data.epi_content:
I opened my eyes in bed, and I was still in this blood-reeking space.

My body had completely healed and was moving and breathing—in other words, there was no problem with being alive.

After getting up from the bed and looking around, what I saw were several bloodstains, shriveled fingers rolling about, and something that was once human but now completely rotten, emitting a terrible smell, constantly catching my eye.

I don't know if they threw me in here to create a sense of terror or if this was just their regular space, but if it's the former, I'd like to give them a round of applause for a job well done.

"Time to let you out now. Since you've entered my private chambers."

With those words, the space began to ripple violently and started to expel me.

Unlike being dragged in, this felt more like being thrown out.

Like being swallowed by a whale and then spat back out.

The Crown Prince wasn't wearing his usual neat school uniform but rather a plain brown casual outfit.

Dressed in clothes comfortable enough to be called sleepwear, he rolled up his sleeves and performed an exaggerated greeting, as if a lower noble welcoming a high noble.

"Whew, welcome to my personal space.

Being in front of others always feels suffocating."

"...Where is this?"

"Haha. Can't you tell? It's my bedroom. It's a bit spacious because I use it for multiple purposes."

Looking around, I could see a bed attached to the far right.

The room was so large that I wasn't sure whether to call it a room or an auditorium.

Anyway, it looked like a bed used solely for sleeping.

It was a bit strange that there were several beds lined up next to each other, but I let it go.

He wasn't the type to answer questions anyway.

There were hooks on the ceiling, perhaps for hanging things, and sadistic-looking tools scattered across the floor. On some furniture, there were things that might have once been human.

With my eyes twitching slightly, I asked about what the Crown Prince would probably call a desk, in a tone of reproach.

"...What, what on earth is that?"

I pointed with a trembling finger at a naked woman carrying a wooden board on her back.

In fact, there wasn't just one such person.

"What else could it be but my one-of-a-kind precious desk? I use it quite often."

"You're insane.

My curse wasn't empty words—you'll ruin this country when you become Emperor.

You goddamn lunatic. How could you do this to people..."

"Why, isn't your family busy committing far worse atrocities on sugar plantations, banana plantations, or cotton fields?

Of course, the person above the landowners has changed to the Imperial family."

"Whatever you do to those Blacks has nothing to do with humans, you lunatic!"

"Ah, here's a surprising fact.

Just as we have brains in our heads, those Blacks you mention are actually people too."

I know that.

But I can't accept it.

Therefore, in my heart, they remain nothing but vulgar and filthy livestock.

The Crown Prince smiled at me, then suddenly approached with heavy footsteps and raised his hand.

I thought he was going to slap me, so I closed my eyes tightly and turned my head...
data.epi_content2:
Thud!

It wasn't my cheek that got hit.

As soon as I closed my eyes, he struck my solar plexus with his left fist.

I couldn't breathe as a result.

And as a bonus, I was vomiting stomach acid since I hadn't eaten anything.

"The desk was initially quite rebellious like you."

When I collapsed clutching my solar plexus, he began kicking me randomly as if he didn't care.

My body, legs, arms, and head—like kicking a soccer ball.

While I thought I might die if I got hit wrong, it didn't take long to realize that was actually a hopeful thought.

Just when I felt like I was about to lose consciousness or stop breathing, black smoke would wrap around my body and heal me completely.

This damn smoke must be why the Crown Prince could survive even with bullets all over his body.

"Whew, I'm getting tired.

I should have built up my stamina, but it was too bothersome.

Anyway, do you feel like choosing your words more carefully now?"

"Your Highness is insane... ugh."

Not only was my lip split, but with every breath I took, the pain suggested that my ribs might have broken and punctured my lung, as blood suddenly flowed from my mouth.

The pain was agonizing, but I was somewhat used to it. Still, I didn't like the pain.

"...In truth, no matter what I say... huff... nothing will change."

"You seem more insane than me—getting beaten like this yet still saying what you want, making a hole in your own throat and trying to shoot me dead.

People usually become extremely conservative when it comes to matters of their own life."

He walks quite a distance to where a chair is.

Then, watching me who had been beaten to dust, he takes out tobacco from his pocket and starts smoking.

Strangely, there's also a pungent soap smell rising, and seeing the Crown Prince's limp body with no strength whatsoever, I suspect he's smoking a mixture of tobacco and opium.

His mouth corners were turned up in a grin, clearly enjoying the sight of me unable to properly control my body.

After inhaling the smoke for a while, perhaps feeling the effects of the drug, he staggered over and collapsed onto the bed.

Then, in a slightly grumbling tone, he spoke while looking at the wall.

"You've been expelled. For inflicting irreparable damage on Lydia, that stupid count's daughter."

Then he curled up on the bed and started laughing hysterically.

"To grind someone's face like that, what a cruel woman you are!"

The Crown Prince, intoxicated by the drug, closed his eyes briefly, but seemingly unable to fall asleep, he approached me again and started trampling me without a word.

My entire body was in pain, my joints screaming for mercy, yet this bizarre feeling of having no actual injuries was unbearably torturous.

"Not screaming even once makes this rather dull and tasteless.

Beg for your life. Most people listen well when beaten like this."

At those words, I spat again.

Spitting also required strength, so it didn't dramatically hit the Crown Prince's face or anything.

It just slightly stained his shoes red.

He lifted his foot and started wiping his dirty shoe on my hair.

It was humiliating.

"No matter how you look at me, I won't die.
data.epi_content3:
How many have I buried so far? All my brothers and sisters looked at me with those same eyes.

All that remains are my lovely family members who listen well to me."

He said this with a smile, then suddenly hardened his expression and stomped on my right eye.

When I closed my left eye, I couldn't see anything.

"AAAAARGH!!!"

My eye hurt so much.

I rubbed my face frantically against the floor.

But the pain wouldn't go away.

"...Don't look at me like that."

No matter how I twisted my body or cursed him, I was powerless.

All I could think of was Evan, who had just watched as I was dragged here.

You said you'd save me. Save me right now. It hurts.

It's painful, sad, miserable, agonizing, and utterly helpless—if not now, when are you coming?

Blood, or tears, soaked my face.

I didn't expect anything from Vivian.

She'd probably forget someone like me in an instant.

With so many impressive and cool people around her.

He mumbled something to the flickering shadow, and it approached me and began to move me somewhere.

"Let's meet again after you've lost some energy."

Somehow already distant from me, he sat on the bed, smiling brightly and waving at me.

………………

I continued to be moved somewhere through the seemingly endless corridors of the Imperial Palace.

And during this movement, I made eye contact with several women wearing pure white dresses.

Their eyes looked as if they were staring at dying, fishy-smelling fish thrown on the floor of a fish market, with no hope of life visible.

Seeing me being dragged somewhere, they followed with their eyes while drinking tea with their hands, as if accustomed to the sight. It made me wonder if they too had been abducted like me.

There was a small waltz playing from somewhere, as if there was a band nearby.

The low, gloomy tones seemed to mock and ridicule me, and I wanted to cover my ears, but my exhausted body couldn't even think of moving a finger.

The palace corridor that seemed endless eventually did have an end, as walls gradually came into view.

The shadow carried me to a room at the very end, opened the door, threw me in, and stuffed something the size of a baseball into my mouth.

Then it left the room and firmly closed the door.

Since it was difficult to breathe through my mouth, I breathed through my nose and looked around the room.

Thinking about it, I realized I hadn't felt much pain when thrown into the room. I gently pressed the floor and walls and found them soft.

Water drops were falling from the ceiling with a drip-drip sound.

The fallen water didn't pool but flowed downward.

There was no bathroom, toilet, or food—nothing.

Just a room surrounded by soft material on all sides with no way to escape through the door.

Faced with the fact that I could do nothing, I just shed tears.

Probably tears of blood.
